boundary_binding.action_boundary: May platform operations activate telemetry service after prerequisite calibration evidence is attached to the activation packet?
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-ACR-022-P: 'Shared naming prefixes are not enough by themselves.' The lab/production distinction and site mismatch prevent closure.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: The calibration evidence (CAL-ACR-022) covers DG-41B lab telemetry units at Site 18-L. The activation (ACT-ACR-022) targets DG-41B production telemetry units at Site 18-P. Per STD-ACR-022-P, shared naming prefixes are insufficient; device group AND site must precisely match. The NOTE-ACR-022 note about shared naming is overridden by the binding policy standard.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: The calibration evidence does not satisfy the activation dependency. The device group designation (lab vs. production) and site (18-L vs. 18-P) create a source-record mismatch that cannot be resolved by naming prefix alone.

final_answer: The action must remain escalated. Calibration packet CAL-ACR-022 covers DG-41B lab telemetry units at Site 18-L, while service activation ACT-ACR-2026-8022 targets DG-41B production telemetry units at Site 18-P. The device group designation (lab vs. production) and site (18-L vs. 18-P) do not match. Per binding policy STD-ACR-022-P, shared naming prefixes are insufficient. The source boundary remains open; activation cannot proceed with mismatched dependency evidence.
open_blockers: CAL-ACR-022 covers DG-41B lab units (Site 18-L); activation targets DG-41B production units (Site 18-P)
Site mismatch: calibration is Site 18-L, activation is Site 18-P
NOTE-ACR-022 shared-prefix note is insufficient per binding policy STD-ACR-022-P
